findings.message: No report found — PO layer skipped. Run 'rivet analyze' first.
No JSON report found — VA layer skipped. Run 'rivet analyze' first.
No JSON report found — VC layer skipped. Run 'rivet analyze' first.

findings.field: Operations
findings.message: SDD doc target unsatisfied: tsg:phase-0.5-source-lift (Operations) (no uncommitted markdown change at docs/TSGs/Operations/phase-0-5-source-lift.md)

findings.field: qBittorrent returns HTTP 200 with body 'Fails.' when credentials are wrong
findings.message: qBittorrent returns HTTP 200 with body 'Fails.' when credentials are wrong: expected _login() raises or sets _logged_in=False so subsequent calls know auth failed, got _login() only calls raise_for_status() (200 passes), sets _logged_in=True even on failed auth, causing infinite 403-retry loops

findings.field: Homebridge login response missing 'access_token' key (schema change or error body with 200 status)
findings.message: Homebridge login response missing 'access_token' key (schema change or error body with 200 status): expected _login() raises or signals failure so get() does not loop re-authenticating every call, got _token is set to None via .get('access_token'), so every subsequent get() re-calls _login() silently, hammering the auth endpoint

findings.field: policy.check_readonly audit callback type signature (3 args) does not match audit.audit function signature (4 args: logger, tool_name, params, result_summary)
findings.message: policy.check_readonly audit callback type signature (3 args) does not match audit.audit function signature (4 args: logger, tool_name, params, result_summary): expected check_readonly can call the audit function provided by the server without TypeError, got audit.audit requires a Logger as first arg; Callable[[str, dict, str], None] annotation in check_readonly expects (tool_name, params, result_summary) — direct passing of audit.audit as the callback raises TypeError at runtime

findings.field: Dockerfile comment says 'container runs as non-root uid 1000' but no USER directive is present
findings.message: Dockerfile comment says 'container runs as non-root uid 1000' but no USER directive is present: expected Container process runs as uid 1000 (non-root) to match the passwd entry and SSH setup, got Without a USER instruction, the ENTRYPOINT runs as root (uid 0); the passwd entry for uid 1000 is unused unless an external orchestrator forces the uid, contradicting the self-documenting intent

findings.field: Write tools call _audit(tool_name, params) with default result_summary='ok' BEFORE _check_readonly, which itself emits a separate 'rejected_readonly' audit entry when HOMELAB_MCP_READONLY=1
findings.message: Write tools call _audit(tool_name, params) with default result_summary='ok' BEFORE _check_readonly, which itself emits a separate 'rejected_readonly' audit entry when HOMELAB_MCP_READONLY=1: expected When readonly mode blocks a write tool, the audit log should contain only a 'rejected_readonly' entry, not a preceding false 'ok' entry, got The audit log contains a misleading 'ok' entry for the operation, followed by a 'rejected_readonly' entry from _check_readonly. Tools like sonarr_search_missing, qbt_pause, dirigera_set_light, unifi_block (and ~15 others) all have _audit() before _check_readonly(), producing false 'ok' entries in the audit trail for operations that never executed.

findings.field: apple_now_playing captures the bound async method `atv.metadata.playing` without calling it, then checks for __await__ on the method object (which is False), so the method is never invoked
findings.message: apple_now_playing captures the bound async method `atv.metadata.playing` without calling it, then checks for __await__ on the method object (which is False), so the method is never invoked: expected The tool should await `atv.metadata.playing()` and return actual playing metadata (title, artist, etc.), got p = atv.metadata.playing assigns the bound method; hasattr(bound_method, '__await__') is False so `p` is never called/awaited. All getattr(p, 'title', None) etc. return None, silently producing empty playing info

findings.field: host_status parses MEM line from `free -m`; if the SSH command partially fails and MEM value is empty or contains non-numeric text, int() conversion raises unhandled ValueError
findings.message: host_status parses MEM line from `free -m`; if the SSH command partially fails and MEM value is empty or contains non-numeric text, int() conversion raises unhandled ValueError: expected Graceful handling when MEM output is empty, malformed, or contains text like 'N/A', got mem_parts = parsed.get('MEM', '0 0 0').split() guards the default, but if SSH returns a line like 'MEM:' (empty value after colon), mem_parts becomes [''] or empty-after-strip. int(mem_parts[0]) on '' raises ValueError that propagates to the caller unhandled.

findings.field: _clients module-level dict is lazily populated by concurrent MCP tool calls without synchronization; two concurrent calls to _sonarr() could both pass the `if 'sonarr' not in _clients` check and overwrite each other
findings.message: _clients module-level dict is lazily populated by concurrent MCP tool calls without synchronization; two concurrent calls to _sonarr() could both pass the `if 'sonarr' not in _clients` check and overwrite each other: expected Each client is instantiated exactly once and shared safely, got In an MCP server handling concurrent tool calls (e.g., homelab_overview calls multiple sub-tools), two threads/tasks may both read _clients['sonarr'] as missing and each create a separate ServarrClient. While Python's GIL prevents memory corruption, it can cause duplicate HTTP sessions and inconsistent per-client state (e.g., auth tokens, session cookies).

findings.field: CF_DEFAULT_ZONE is set to a domain not in CF_ALLOWED_ZONES
findings.message: CF_DEFAULT_ZONE is set to a domain not in CF_ALLOWED_ZONES: expected cf_default_zone() should raise ConfigurationError or at least validate the explicit value is a member of cf_allowed_zones(), got cf_default_zone() only validates domain syntax, never checks membership in cf_allowed_zones(). A caller trusting cf_default_zone() as pre-authorized can operate on an unauthorized zone.

findings.field: HOMELAB_MCP_READONLY env var has trailing whitespace (e.g. 'true ' from YAML or .env)
findings.message: HOMELAB_MCP_READONLY env var has trailing whitespace (e.g. 'true ' from YAML or .env): expected env_flag('HOMELAB_MCP_READONLY') returns True because the intent is truthy, got env_flag does not call .strip() on the value, so 'true ' is not in ('1', 'true', 'yes') and the function returns False, silently disabling readonly mode. require_env strips but env_flag does not.

findings.field: Subdomain label with trailing hyphen (e.g. 'foo-.cluster.example') is tested against ingress SSRF allowlist
findings.message: Subdomain label with trailing hyphen (e.g. 'foo-.cluster.example') is tested against ingress SSRF allowlist: expected ingress_host_re() rejects 'foo-.cluster.example' because trailing hyphens violate RFC 1035 label rules, got ingress_host_re() builds pattern ^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9-]*\.{domain}$ which allows [a-z0-9-]* to end with a hyphen. The _LABEL_RE on line ~100 that correctly enforces no-trailing-hyphen is dead code (never referenced).

findings.field: test_ingress_host_re_escapes_dots_in_domain uses input 'sonarrXclusterXexample' which is rejected by the literal \. separator, not by domain dot escaping
findings.message: test_ingress_host_re_escapes_dots_in_domain uses input 'sonarrXclusterXexample' which is rejected by the literal \. separator, not by domain dot escaping: expected The test should fail if re.escape(domain) is replaced with just domain (i.e., dots in the domain become wildcards), got Input 'sonarrXclusterXexample' fails at the hard-coded \. before the domain (X≠dot), so the test passes with or without re.escape. Use 'sonarr.clusterXexample' instead—without re.escape the unescaped '.' in 'cluster.example' would match 'X', producing a false allow.

findings.field: _is_in_docstring in test_no_homelab_specifics_in_source.py counts triple-quote occurrences, but string literals containing '"""' corrupt the count
findings.message: _is_in_docstring in test_no_homelab_specifics_in_source.py counts triple-quote occurrences, but string literals containing '"""' corrupt the count: expected A private IP on a line after a string literal containing triple-quotes (e.g. x='"""') is detected as NOT inside a docstring, got _is_in_docstring counts all '"""' substrings before the match position. A string literal like x='"""' increments the count to odd, so subsequent real code is misclassified as 'inside a docstring' and the private-data leak is silently skipped.

findings.field: Each tool invocation constructs a new ServarrClient/QbtClient/Mylar3Client/PlexClient/HomebridgeClient instance under load.
findings.message: Each tool invocation constructs a new ServarrClient/QbtClient/Mylar3Client/PlexClient/HomebridgeClient instance under load.: expected HTTP connections are deterministically released (close/Dispose) when the client is no longer needed., got clients.py creates httpx.Client instances but exposes no close() and never disposes them, so repeated instantiation can accumulate open sockets/file descriptors.

findings.field: UNIFI_PORT is set but empty (common with misconfigured secret/env injection).
findings.message: UNIFI_PORT is set but empty (common with misconfigured secret/env injection).: expected Port parsing should safely fall back to 443 (or emit a controlled config error)., got get_unifi_config() does int(env('UNIFI_PORT', '443')); when env is empty string, int('') raises ValueError and crashes startup.

findings.field: Homebridge login endpoint returns 200 but omits access_token (empty/malformed auth payload).
findings.message: Homebridge login endpoint returns 200 but omits access_token (empty/malformed auth payload).: expected _login() should reject missing token immediately with a clear auth error., got HomebridgeClient._login() assigns None to self._token, then get() sends Authorization: Bearer None and fails later with misleading HTTP errors.

findings.field: kube_image_can_pull is called with an image that is pullable, but the test pod (`kubectl run ... -- /bin/true`) exits immediately.
findings.message: kube_image_can_pull is called with an image that is pullable, but the test pod (`kubectl run ... -- /bin/true`) exits immediately.: expected Successful image pull should return `{ "can_pull": true }` even for short-lived pods., got The code waits for `condition=Ready`; short-lived pods often transition directly to `Succeeded` and never become Ready, causing false `can_pull: false` failures.

findings.field: cf_dns_list is used on a zone containing more than 200 DNS records.
findings.message: cf_dns_list is used on a zone containing more than 200 DNS records.: expected Function should paginate Cloudflare responses and return all records as promised by the docstring., got Only one request is made with `per_page=200`, so records after page 1 are silently dropped.

findings.field: image_list_tags is called with `limit=0` (or a negative limit).
findings.message: image_list_tags is called with `limit=0` (or a negative limit).: expected Boundary values should return zero tags (or be rejected as invalid)., got Slicing uses `tags[-limit:]`; for `limit=0`, Python returns all tags (`tags[0:]`), violating the limit contract.

findings.field: Dirigera initialization fails once due a transient error (network/import issue), then configuration becomes healthy.
findings.message: Dirigera initialization fails once due a transient error (network/import issue), then configuration becomes healthy.: expected Subsequent calls should retry initialization and recover., got Exception path stores `_clients['dirigera'] = None`; future calls return cached None without retry, causing permanent failure until process restart.

findings.field: kube_image_present runs on a host without crictl, triggering command fallback `|| echo '[]'`.
findings.message: kube_image_present runs on a host without crictl, triggering command fallback `|| echo '[]'`.: expected Empty fallback should be handled as no cached images and return `{present: false}` without internal parsing errors., got Parsed JSON is a list (`[]`), but code unconditionally calls `data.get(...)`; this raises `'list' object has no attribute get'` and returns an error payload.

findings.field: Operator sets CF_ALLOWED_ZONES=internal.example and CF_DEFAULT_ZONE=prod.example
findings.message: Operator sets CF_ALLOWED_ZONES=internal.example and CF_DEFAULT_ZONE=prod.example: expected Configuration should fail fast because default zone is outside the allowlist contract, got cf_default_zone() returns CF_DEFAULT_ZONE without checking membership in cf_allowed_zones(), enabling an out-of-policy zone selection

findings.field: Run tools/lift_phase_0_5.py with --dry-run on a clean checkout
findings.message: Run tools/lift_phase_0_5.py with --dry-run on a clean checkout: expected Dry-run should not create or modify any files/directories, got main() always executes sdd_dir.mkdir(...) even in dry-run mode, causing filesystem writes despite 'writes nothing' contract

findings.field: Destination repo already contains mcp/src/old_removed.py, but source repo no longer tracks it
findings.message: Destination repo already contains mcp/src/old_removed.py, but source repo no longer tracks it: expected Lift should produce an exact mirror or fail on stale destination files, got lift_phase_0_5.py only copies tracked source files and never deletes stale destination files, leaving drift that can ship obsolete code

findings.field: scan_server_tools() returns duplicate tool names due accidental duplicate decorator definitions
findings.message: scan_server_tools() returns duplicate tool names due accidental duplicate decorator definitions: expected verify_lift should fail and report duplicate tool names explicitly, got verify_lift.py converts scanned and inventory names to sets, silently collapsing duplicates and potentially passing a broken lifted server
